task_completion.description: Change the 'dose' variable to nominal in JASP
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the JASP data view, the column header for 'dose' should display an icon indicating it is a nominal/categorical variable (e.g., three overlapping circles/Venn diagram), not a scale variable (ruler).

task_completion.description: Configure Naive Bayes classification with correct target and predictors
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the JASP Machine Learning -> Classification -> Naive Bayes settings panel, 'dose' should be placed in the Target box, and 'len' and 'supp' should be placed in the Features/Predictors box.

task_completion.description: Set the validation method and random seed
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Data Split section of the Naive Bayes settings, either 'Training and test split' is set to 30% test (or 70% training) OR 'Cross-validation' is set to 5 folds. The 'Set seed' checkbox must be checked with the value 42.

task_completion.description: Display the confusion matrix and classification accuracy
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JASP results panel should display a 'Naive Bayes Classification' section containing an 'Evaluation Metrics' table with Accuracy (between 0.40 and 0.95) and a 'Confusion Matrix' showing classes 500, 1000, and 2000.
A 'Save As' dialog shows the file being saved as 'NaiveBayesDose.jasp' in the /home/ga/Documents/JASP/ folder, or the JASP main window title shows 'NaiveBayesDose.jasp'.

task_completion.description: Create the text report with required contents
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor window shows a file named 'naive_bayes_report.txt'. The text must include the algorithm name ('Naive Bayes'), the accuracy value, confusion matrix counts, and a sentence interpreting which dose level (500, 1000, or 2000) was easiest or hardest to classify.

integrity.description: The agent used the JASP GUI to perform the analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the JASP application interface being interacted with to configure the model and view results, not terminal commands or Python scripts bypassing the GUI.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI text editor to create the report
integrity.visual_evidence: The report should be visible in a GUI text editor (like Mousepad, Gedit, or Nano in a terminal window), rather than being created invisibly via echo/cat commands.

integrity.description: The report contents match the JASP results
integrity.visual_evidence: The accuracy and confusion matrix values written in the text report should match the actual output displayed in the JASP results panel.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target variable 'dose' must be treated as nominal. The actual class levels for 'dose' in the JASP dataset are 500, 1000, and 2000. Expected Naive Bayes accuracy is between 0.40 and 0.95 (typically 0.66-0.70). The predictors are 'len' and 'supp'.
